about.intro: I'm Rabin R — a Freelance Angular & Full Stack Developer who helps startups and enterprises ship production-ready web and mobile applications that are fast, scalable, and maintainable.
bio: With 4+ years of professional experience, I build responsive, high-performance applications using Angular 17–21, TypeScript, RxJS, Angular Signals, Node.js, Ionic, and REST APIs. I have delivered enterprise-grade systems including government immigration management portals, pension management platforms, and mobile-first applications used by thousands of users across multiple countries.

Currently freelancing and open to remote engagements — I bring senior-level Angular architecture thinking to every project. My focus is on clean, reusable component systems, performance optimization (Core Web Vitals, lazy loading, change detection), secure API integration, and delivering UI experiences that users actually love.

experience.title: A track record of shipping enterprise software
experience.subtitle: From government immigration platforms to large multi-step pension applications, here is where my craft has been forged.

achievements: Delivering end-to-end Angular web applications for international clients
Architecting scalable frontend systems with Angular 19–21 and standalone components
Building performant dashboards, admin panels, and enterprise UI systems
Integrating REST APIs, JWT authentication, and role-based access control (RBAC)
Developing cross-platform mobile apps using Ionic Angular & Capacitor
Providing performance audits and Angular migration from legacy versions
Collaborating with global teams using Agile workflows and Git-based CI/CD
Developed enterprise Angular applications for government and business platforms
Built scalable Single Page Applications (SPA) used by thousands of daily active users
Integrated secure REST APIs and JWT authentication across 5+ enterprise systems
Improved application performance by 40% using Angular Signals and RxJS optimisation
Developed reusable frontend architecture and UI systems adopted across 3 product lines
Delivered responsive cross-platform interfaces for desktop, tablet, and mobile
Worked in Agile/Scrum enterprise environments with global stakeholders
Optimised API requests and frontend rendering performance reducing load time by 35%

skills.title: A modern toolkit, mastered in production

projects.title: Selected work — enterprise, government & mobile
projects.subtitle: A peek at recent products I've helped design, engineer, and ship. Each one balances scalable architecture with refined UI.

featured.name: Fiji Immigration Department Portal
featured.description: Enterprise immigration management platform developed for government workflows including external user portals, internal staff portals, police verification systems, and secure payment gateway integration.
featured.highlights: Multi-step enterprise workflow systems, Secure authentication & RBAC, Responsive government portal UI, High-performance Angular architecture, REST API integration.

caseStudy.problem: The Fiji Immigration Department relied on outdated paper-based workflows and fragmented legacy systems, causing processing delays of 3–5 business days per application. Staff had no unified digital view of applicant status, resulting in manual follow-ups and high error rates.
caseStudy.approach: Built a multi-portal Angular 21 SPA with role-based access control — separate portals for external applicants, internal immigration officers, and police verification units. Used Angular Signals for real-time UI state and integrated a secure payment gateway for permit fees.
challenges: Complex multi-role RBAC with 6 distinct user types across 3 separate portal UIs
Integrating legacy government APIs with inconsistent data formats
Ensuring WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance for a government system

grid.alt: PRIMS — Pension & Retirement Information Management System
grid.name: PRIMS — Pension & Retirement Information Management System
grid.description: High-performance pension and retirement dashboard system designed for enterprise administration and scalable data workflows.
grid.highlights: Zoneless Angular architecture, Optimized rendering strategies, Dashboard analytics UI, API optimization & caching, Reusable enterprise components.

caseStudy.problem: Legacy pension system had critical performance bottlenecks — dashboard rendered 1,200+ records without virtualisation, causing 8-second load times on mid-range hardware used by administrators.
caseStudy.approach: Rebuilt the dashboard with Angular 21 Zoneless change detection, CDK virtual scroll for large datasets, and an API caching layer using RxJS shareReplay. Introduced reusable enterprise component library for consistent UI across 12 dashboard modules.
challenges: Migrating from Angular 14 zone-based to Angular 21 zoneless without breaking existing RBAC logic
Handling 50,000+ pension records with real-time filtering and sorting
Coordinating with legacy backend teams on API contract standardisation

grid.name: VNPF — Vanuatu National Provident Fund
grid.description: Cross-platform mobile application developed using Ionic Angular with biometric authentication and optimised mobile workflows.
grid.highlights: Android & iOS support, Face ID & Touch ID integration, Responsive mobile-first design, Low-bandwidth API optimisation, Hybrid mobile application architecture.

caseStudy.problem: Fund members in Vanuatu had no mobile access to their provident fund accounts. Manual branch visits were the only way to check balances, causing long queues and poor member experience, especially on outer islands with limited connectivity.
caseStudy.approach: Built a cross-platform Ionic Angular app (Android + iOS) with offline-capable data caching for low-bandwidth environments. Integrated Capacitor biometric plugin for Face ID / Touch ID authentication and optimised API payloads for 2G/3G conditions.
challenges: Implementing secure biometric authentication that degrades gracefully on older Android devices
Designing offline mode with reliable cache invalidation for balance data
App Store and Google Play submission and compliance review cycles

hero.title: Let's Build Something
hero.subtitle: Whether you need a new Angular app from scratch, a performance overhaul, or an Ionic mobile app — I bring senior-level thinking to every engagement.

process.title: How we work together
process.subtitle: A transparent, structured process so you always know what's happening — no surprises.

steps.title: Discovery Call
steps.desc: 30-minute call to understand your project goals, technical requirements, timeline, and budget. No commitment required.

steps.title: Proposal & Scope
steps.desc: I'll send a detailed proposal with scope, milestones, timeline, and fixed-price or retainer quote within 48 hours.

steps.title: Build & Iterate
steps.desc: Weekly progress updates, Loom walkthroughs of new features, and a staging URL so you can see progress in real-time.

steps.title: Launch & Handoff
steps.desc: Production deployment, documentation, knowledge transfer, and 30 days of post-launch support included.

faqs.q: Do you work on fixed-price or hourly rates?
faqs.a: Both. For well-defined projects I prefer fixed-price with clear milestones. For ongoing work or evolving requirements, a monthly retainer works best. I'll recommend the right model during the discovery call.

faqs.q: What timezone do you work in?
faqs.a: I'm in IST (UTC+5:30) but I regularly collaborate with clients in Europe, Australia, North America, and Africa. I schedule overlap hours to match your team's working hours.
Yes, absolutely. I sign NDAs before any sensitive project discussion. Client confidentiality is non-negotiable.

faqs.q: Can you work with our existing team?
faqs.a: Yes. I'm comfortable joining as an embedded contractor in your Jira, Slack, GitHub workflow — or working independently and reporting to a product manager.

faqs.q: What if the project scope changes?
faqs.a: Scope changes happen. Any significant additions outside the agreed scope are quoted separately with your approval before work begins — no surprise invoices.
Every project includes 30 days of post-launch support for bug fixes. Extended support retainers are available for ongoing maintenance.

notAFit: Projects with no technical brief or undefined requirements
Projects requiring 100% guaranteed launch within 1 week
